chosen_action.title: Prevent re-queuing already-contacted publisher targets during active review windows

shared_findings_used: MARKETING_SELF_IMPROVEMENT.md: outcomes matter more than activity
MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: flat tactics must be replaced, not rewarded for volume
FOUR_MARKETING_QUESTIONS.md: Codeberg-first framing remains the target outcome surface
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: current bottleneck is conversion from interest to free use
market_intelligence_latest.json: reusable publisher/comparison positioning truths already exist
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.md: same-family outreach overlap is already a failing tactic
reddit_monitor_latest.md: Reddit remains partial/blocked, so this run should avoid fake progress there
reddit_post_analysis.json: historical repetition risk remains parked while the channel is blocked
adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg is still flat and remains the primary success gate
distribution_lane_latest.json: the loop was in measurement_hold because fresh external actions already shipped
primary_repo_flat_contact_discovery_latest.json: AXME Code and WyeWorks were already among the discovered publisher targets
why_this_action: The current bottleneck is still primary-repo conversion, but the loop had fresh evidence that publisher outreach had already shipped to discovered targets inside the same review window. Without a guard, the primary-repo-flat contact lane could keep regenerating or reusing packets for already-contacted publishers, creating overlap noise instead of cleaner Codeberg conversion measurement.

behavior: Selector now subtracts publisher targets that already received recent publisher_email_outreach before deciding whether a fresh primary_repo_flat_contact_handoff_packet is still actionable.
Selector now records explicit reasons when all discovered publisher targets are already inside their fresh outreach review windows.
Executor now filters recently-contacted publisher targets out of the primary-repo-flat handoff packet and preserves follow-through when the remaining actionable set is empty.
Marketing execution board now hides publisher-contact targets that already received fresh outreach, reducing fake outstanding-work signals.
Regression tests now cover both selector and executor behavior for recently-contacted publisher targets.

runtime_result.reason: Several fresh external actions already shipped in the short review window, and the other executable lanes are still inside measurement or handoff windows; hold for follow-through instead of inventing another reset.
notable_reasons: Fresh publisher-contact targets remain, but the short review window already has enough live external actions that another contact packet now would blur measurement more than it helps.
Fresh publisher outreach already shipped in the current 7-day review window, so those targets should not be re-queued immediately.

expected_outcome: Cleaner measurement around the already-sent publisher outreach, fewer duplicate packet refreshes, and less same-window overlap pressure on the Codeberg-primary contact lane.
measurement_window: Use the next 7 days to watch for replies/citations from the already-contacted publishers and confirm the loop does not regenerate them as fresh actionable targets during that window.
replacement_condition: If publisher-contact targets still reappear as actionable during the same review window, extend the guard to channel-specific contact logs or add stricter per-target cooldown state.
